steps.title: Summarize the findings
steps.content: The notes distinguish syllable rate from information rate. Syllable rates varied across languages, as shown by Spanish and Vietnamese, but the information rate was approximately consistent across all 17 languages.

steps.title: Reject narrower summaries
steps.content: Choice B names what the researchers measured but not the key finding. Choice C invents a lower information rate for Spanish. Choice D confuses syllables per second with information conveyed per second. The answer is A.
